textPlain:
As much as it'll be "interesting" to see how models behave in real world examples (presumably similarly to how the demos went), I'm not convinced this is a premade recording like what seems to be implied.I'm imagining this is an incomplete flow within a software prototype that may have jumped steps and lacks sufficient multi-modal capability to correct.It could also be staged recordings.  
But, I don't think it really matters.  Models are easily capable of working with the setup and flow they have for the demo.  It's real world accuracy, latency, convenience, and other factors that will impact actual users the most.What's the reliability and latency needed for these to be a useful tool?For example, I can't imagine many people wanting to use the gesture writing tools for most messages.  It's cool, I like that it was developed, but I doubt it'll see substantial adoption with what's currently being pitched.
There is a second part that is equally bad, but with Zuck:https://old.reddit.com/r/interestingasfuck/comments/1nkbqyk/...
More than likely the full response was kept as context despite being interrupted.Notably though, the AI was clearly not utilizing its visual feed to work alongside him as implied
Never work with children, animals, and puppets.https://tvtropes.org/pmwiki/pmwiki.php/Main/NeverWorkWithChi...
I bet they rehearsed a dozen times and never failed as bad live. Got to give them props for keeping the live demos. Apple has neutered its demos so much it's now basically 2 hr long commercials.
Should've downloaded more ram for the wifi to work better
I love how they randomly blame the WiFi network, like anyone is going to buy it.

That wasn't prerecorded, but it was rigged. They probably practiced a few times and it confused the AI. Still it's no excuse. They've dropped Apollo-program level money on this and it's still dumb as a rock.I'm endless amazed that Meta has a ~2T market cap, yet they can't build products.
This is like a Black Mirror episode. Also, is it a conscious decision to make the TTS sound so robotic?
Those WiFi's man, they're always trouble
What a piece of crap. The glasses don't seem to work very well either.
AI is hot trash. When will this river of garbage stop?
[dead]
The failures on stage were kind of endearing, to be honest, especially the one with Zuck. Plus the products seem really cool, I hope I'll be able to try them out soon.

Yea the behavior of the AI read to me more like a hard coded demo but still very much "live". I suspect him cutting it off was poorly timed and that timing could have amplified due to WiFi? Who knows. I wasn't there. I didn't build it.
God, that's actually painful to watch. I can't believe I lasted two minutes.
Would be good to change the OP link to this - it's the same clip but plus a bit more.
It's almost certainly a joke. Everyone knows that the demo failed.
probably a reference to Steve Jobs or an attempted joke ?
Well, it _IS_ a rock after all.
[delayed]
These hot takes with no context only make the ai argument stronger.

Zuckerberg has negative charisma, it's painful to watch...Jobs handled this so much better; while clearly he is pissed, he doesn't leave you cringing in mutual embarrassment, goes to show it isn't as easy as he makes it look!See: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1M4t14s7nSM
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=znxQOPFg2mo
Yeah. I’m impressed we have any sort of wave guide display on sale commercially this year.
